concepts.name: Function
concepts.short_definition: A relation between a set of inputs and a set of permissible outputs with the property that each input is related to exactly one output.

concepts.name: Graph of a function
concepts.short_definition: A visual representation of a function, plotting input values against their corresponding output values.

concepts.name: Coordinate plane
concepts.short_definition: A two-dimensional plane defined by two perpendicular axes (x-axis and y-axis) used to locate points.

concepts.name: Interval
concepts.short_definition: A continuous set of numbers between two given numbers.

concepts.name: Area
concepts.short_definition: The measure of the extent of a two-dimensional surface.

concepts.name: Geometric shapes
concepts.short_definition: Standard two-dimensional figures with defined properties, like rectangles and triangles.

concepts.name: Rectangle area
concepts.short_definition: The calculation of the space enclosed by a rectangle, typically length times width.

concepts.name: Approximation
concepts.short_definition: Finding a value that is close to the true value.

concepts.name: Summation
concepts.short_definition: The addition of a sequence of numbers.

concepts.name: Limit
concepts.short_definition: The value that a function or sequence 'approaches' as the input or index approaches some value.

concepts.name: Riemann sum
concepts.short_definition: An approximation of the area under a curve by dividing the area into a series of rectangles.

concepts.name: Left Riemann sum
concepts.short_definition: A Riemann sum where the height of each rectangle is determined by the function's value at the left endpoint of its subinterval.

concepts.name: Right Riemann sum
concepts.short_definition: A Riemann sum where the height of each rectangle is determined by the function's value at the right endpoint of its subinterval.

concepts.name: Midpoint Riemann sum
concepts.short_definition: A Riemann sum where the height of each rectangle is determined by the function's value at the midpoint of its subinterval.

concepts.name: Subinterval
concepts.short_definition: A smaller interval within a larger interval, used to divide the area under a curve.

concepts.name: Width of subinterval
concepts.short_definition: The length of each subinterval in a Riemann sum.

concepts.name: Number of rectangles
concepts.short_definition: The quantity of rectangles used in a Riemann sum approximation.

concepts.name: Definite integral
concepts.short_definition: The exact area under a curve between two specified points on the x-axis.

concepts.name: Integral symbol
concepts.short_definition: The elongated 'S' symbol used to denote integration.

concepts.name: Integrand
concepts.short_definition: The function that is being integrated.

concepts.name: Limits of integration
concepts.short_definition: The upper and lower values of the interval over which integration is performed.

concepts.name: Antiderivative
concepts.short_definition: A function whose derivative is the given function.

concepts.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
concepts.short_definition: A theorem that links differentiation and integration, stating that the definite integral of a function can be found by evaluating its antiderivative at the limits of integration.

concepts.name: FTC Part 1
concepts.short_definition: Relates differentiation and integration by showing that the derivative of an integral is the original function.

concepts.name: FTC Part 2
concepts.short_definition: Provides a method for evaluating definite integrals using antiderivatives.

concepts.name: Area between curves
concepts.short_definition: The region bounded by two or more functions.

concepts.name: Integration techniques
concepts.short_definition: Various strategies used to find antiderivatives of complex functions.

concepts.name: Substitution rule
concepts.short_definition: A technique for integration that involves changing the variable of integration.

concepts.name: Integration by parts
concepts.short_definition: A technique for integration that is derived from the product rule for differentiation.

concepts.name: Improper integral
concepts.short_definition: A definite integral where one or both limits of integration are infinite, or where the integrand has an infinite discontinuity within the interval of integration.

concepts.name: Area of revolution
concepts.short_definition: The surface area generated by revolving a curve around an axis.

concepts.name: Volume of revolution
concepts.short_definition: The volume of a solid generated by revolving a two-dimensional shape around an axis.

concepts.name: Disk method
concepts.short_definition: A method for calculating the volume of a solid of revolution by integrating the areas of infinitesimally thin disks.

concepts.name: Washer method
concepts.short_definition: A method for calculating the volume of a solid of revolution with a hole in the center, by integrating the areas of infinitesimally thin washers.

concepts.name: Shell method
concepts.short_definition: A method for calculating the volume of a solid of revolution by integrating the surface areas of infinitesimally thin cylindrical shells.

concepts.name: Numerical integration
concepts.short_definition: Methods for approximating the value of a definite integral when an analytical solution is difficult or impossible to find.

concepts.name: Trapezoidal rule
concepts.short_definition: A numerical integration technique that approximates the area under a curve by dividing it into trapezoids.

concepts.name: Simpson's rule
concepts.short_definition: A numerical integration technique that approximates the area under a curve using parabolic segments.

concepts.name: Derivative
concepts.short_definition: The instantaneous rate at which a function's value changes with respect to its input.
